manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

notable

Related words are:

- important
- outstanding
- prominent

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

content.thinking:
I need to describe the word "notable" without using:
- notable (or its variants like notably)
- important
- outstanding
- prominent

I need to think of synonyms or ways to describe "notable" that don't use these words.

Notable means worthy of attention, remarkable, significant, distinguished, noteworthy.

I could use:
- remarkable
- significant
- worthy of attention
- distinguished
- famous
- well-known
- memorable
- striking

Let me give a short, punchy clue:

"Worthy of attention or notice; remarkable"

Wait, let me make it even shorter and clearer:

"Remarkable and worthy of notice"

or

"Deserving attention; memorable"

or

"Worth mentioning or remembering"

content.text: CLUE: Worthy of attention or recognition; remarkable and deserving to be mentioned

clem_player.response: CLUE: Worthy of attention or recognition; remarkable and deserving to be mentioned

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to guess a target word that another player describes to you.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

After each trial you will get a new hint from the other player which starts with CLUE.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: Worthy of attention or recognition; remarkable and deserving to be mentioned

content.thinking:
The clue describes something that is "worthy of attention or recognition; remarkable and deserving to be mentioned."

This sounds like the word "noteworthy" or "notable" or "remarkable" itself.

Let me think about words that mean deserving to be mentioned:
- noteworthy
- notable
- remarkable
- mentionable
- significant
- important

The phrase "deserving to be mentioned" is key here. The word "mention" is in the clue. Words related to "mention" include:
- mentionable
- noteworthy (note = to mention/record)
- notable

I think "noteworthy" is the most likely answer as it directly combines "note" (to mention/record) with "worthy" and perfectly matches the definition given.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to guess a target word that another player describes to you.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

After each trial you will get a new hint from the other player which starts with CLUE.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: Worthy of attention or recognition; remarkable and deserving to be mentioned
CLUE: Famous or distinguished; also describes a person of high rank or achievement

content.thinking:
The first clue was "Worthy of attention or recognition; remarkable and deserving to be mentioned" and I guessed "noteworthy" which was apparently incorrect.

Now the second clue is "Famous or distinguished; also describes a person of high rank or achievement"

This could be words like:
- notable
- eminent
- prominent
- distinguished
- renowned
- illustrious
- prestigious

The word "notable" seems to fit both clues well:
1. First clue: worthy of attention, remarkable, deserving to be mentioned - yes, this fits "notable"
2. Second clue: famous or distinguished, person of high rank or achievement - yes, this also fits "notable"

Actually, "notable" and "noteworthy" are very similar in meaning, but "notable" can be used as both an adjective and a noun (referring to a person of distinction), while "noteworthy" is primarily an adjective.

Given the second clue specifically mentions "describes a person of high rank," I think "notable" is the answer since it can be used as a noun to refer to such a person.
